sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports a headache that has been worsening recently, accompanied by elevated blood pressure readings over the past three weeks. She describes experiencing headaches approximately once a week and reports taking Tylenol for relief. She denies nasal congestion or cough. She reports blood sugar levels ranging from 100 to 120. She has been walking regularly.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Blood pressure readings have been elevated, ranging up to 120/82 mmHg. No frontal or maxillary sinus tenderness was noted. No lymphadenopathy was identified. Neck examination revealed no tenderness upon palpation.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient presents with a headache and elevated blood pressure, potentially related to hypertension. Further investigation is warranted to determine the etiology of the headaches.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: Increase lisinopril to 40 mg once daily. Order blood work including hemoglobin, A1c, and laboratory tests. Provide diabetic education. Schedule follow-up to assess blood pressure control and headache severity.
